core_mechanism: a complete-data event-history (counting-process) likelihood for a system of coupled continuous-time stochastic jump processes observed only partially, closed under data augmentation over the latent event trajectories so that the intractable partial-observation likelihood is recovered by marginalizing the completed history
brief_reason: The abstract's principal contribution is deriving a complete-data event-history likelihood for interacting continuous-time processes (state progression, network evolution, observation) under partial observation that supports likelihood-based and Bayesian inference via data augmentation over latent events.
